programme.title: Marketplace
programme.desc: Canada's consumer watchdog series since 1972, this news program uncovers wrongdoings in the marketplace. With the help of hidden cameras, correspondents dish out the dirt on not so "clean" business practices. Once they get the evidence they need on tape, they apply pressure on businesses to change policies vis-a-vis the products and services they provide to the public.

programme.title: About That

programme.title: The Fifth Estate
programme.desc: The top-notch team of hosts Bob McKeown, Gillian Findlay, Mark Kelley and Habiba Nosheen provides unique and engaging documentaries from Canada and beyond. "The Fifth Estate" is a reference to the term Fourth Estate, and was chosen to highlight the program's determination to go beyond everyday news into original journalism. The series has won numerous awards for its reporting, including Gemini Awards, International Emmys, the Michener Award, and even an Academy Award for a 1981 episode that was released in theaters the following year. Since September 1975, Canadians have tuned in to experience interesting stories that are always well-crafted and thought-provoking.

programme.title: Alpine Lakes - Dark Depths and Icy Heights
programme.desc: This is a nature documentary, which leads us into the fascinating world of deep mountain lakes. We conquer ice palaces of unsuspected beauty. In the freezers, in which elves, fairies and mountain trolls once did their mischief, we move, using a special breathing technique and with special cameras in search of nocturnal hunters, whose eyes are equipped with residual light amplifying receptors.

programme.title: 15th Meridian East - Similarity in Difference
programme.desc: What ideas are people on the 15th meridian east developing to shape the future and leave tomorrow's generations a home worth living in? This consequence leads us to four future designers. From the barren Namib Desert to the glacier worlds of Spitsbergen, Sicily and the Croatian Mediterranean.

programme.title: 15th Meridian East - Similarity in Difference
programme.desc: The journey leads from the Norwegian Vesterålen to Chad, the Aeolian Islands and the Polish Giant Mountains. As different as the landscapes and cultures may be, all people are united in their love for their children and their desire to give something to tomorrow's generation.

programme.title: mareTV: The Eckernförde Bay - Beach shooters, mine clearance divers, squirrels
programme.desc: Fjord, ford, bay, gulf: there are many names for the beautiful extensions of the Baltic Sea into the country. Eckernförde Bay is shaped by currents, surf and people. It stretches 17 kilometers to the town that gives it its name. Stephan Möller is the acting shooting master of the Eckernförder Bürgerschützengilde. He spent a whole year preparing the beach shooting festival and then this: fog, rain and a stiff breeze. Traditionally, the Eckernförde marksmen always shoot at a wooden bird in the direction of the sea at Whitsun. Under strict conditions! And these stipulate a clear view of at least one kilometer out into the bay. The event literally threatens to fall through, for the first time in 449 years. Around 3,000 soldiers are stationed at the Eckernförde naval base, but only a small group is allowed to call itself an elite unit: the mine divers. They are considered the toughest soldiers in the Navy. Anyone who wants to be one of them has to undergo extreme training. This year, just two candidates made it to the final round. Now they still have to pass the dreaded final exam: running 20 kilometers with marching gear and then swimming ten kilometers through Eckernförde Bay.

programme.title: mareTV: Wismar Bay - World Heritage and Water Paradise
programme.desc: The Wismar Bay is unique in its diversity on the Mecklenburg Baltic Sea coast: cliffs, sandy beaches, salt marshes, enchanted islands and, last but not least, the proud Hanseatic city of Wismar with its brick Gothic buildings, which are a UNESCO World Heritage Site. When the mighty logs get wedged together in the timber mill in the port of Wismar, Steven Buchholz has to get to work with his chainsaw. It's a dangerous job, because the logs weigh tons. As a trained carpenter, Steven is a virtuoso with the chain saw. Even after his shift is over, he is looking for the thrill: the wind off his home island of Poel is favorable that day and he wants to reclaim the German record in the kite surfing high jump. For a long time, Ramona Stelzer only knew fish as a tasty ingredient between two halves of a bun. But when the goldsmith got her hands on fish leather, her business idea was born: she processes the unique natural product fish skin into fine jewelry. The problem is that hardly anyone in Germany tans fish skin. In order to reliably obtain supplies, Ramona now wants to learn this craft herself.

programme.title: Lunenburg: Where the Land Meets the Sea
programme.desc: On the east coast of Canada, a place whose name sounds strangely familiar. The small town of Lunenburg, west of Halifax, was founded in 1753 by North German immigrants. Today, the idyllic town with its many wooden houses is a UNESCO World Heritage Site. In the 19th century, Lunenburg was one of the richest towns in the British Empire, becoming prosperous through fishing and shipbuilding. Glenn Rhodenizer's family traces back ten generations directly to German immigrants. And what the Rhodenizers primarily grow on their fields directly by the sea could hardly be more typical: it is white cabbage, which the farming family processes into sauerkraut. Sauerkraut can be found in every restaurant and supermarket in this area; it is the specialty of the region. The heritage of wooden boat building is successfully maintained by David Westergard. In his ancient shed, he is assembling a 20-meter schooner from four different types of local wood. Building wooden boats, he says, is like "slow food": sustainable, conscious, environmentally friendly, waste-free.

programme.title: Elephants: Back to the Wild
programme.sub-title: Elephants: Back to the Wild: The Return of the Elephants - Tsavo-East-Nationalpark
programme.desc: We accompany the first steps of a two-year-old elephant who is just starting his release program. We also observe the story of two young elephants in their first night in the wilderness on their own. Captivating film footage allows us to truly learn how elephants behave in the wild.

programme.title: Missing Butterflies
programme.desc: Its miraculous transformation from caterpillar to remarkable winged beauty has been a point of constant intrigue. However, in the last few years, many have observed a decrease in the butterfly population. A much-discussed topic in the entomological society is the decline of the insect populace, but to what extent is this topic relevant to our beloved butterflies? For years, a teacher from lower Bavaria and a biologist from Munich have dedicated themselves to studying the butterfly and their presence in Bavaria. The results of their studies tell an enlightening story that is sure to entertain.

programme.title: Rewilding: Back to Nature
programme.desc: Worldwide, there are various projects and approaches to give nature more space again. The aim is to create larger wilderness areas that develop with hardly any influence of humans - by reintroducing so-called key animal species and creating a network of wilderness corridors across entire continents. Rewilding promises to be a cost-effective form of conservation in which we can boost biodiversity and protect the climate.

programme.title: New Life
programme.desc: In a land of dangers, plant-eating animals are always at risk, and never more so than at the time of their birth; out on the open they are vulnerable and there are few places to hide. But luckily some are born to run …

programme.title: New Life
programme.desc: It's a water world and life couldn't survive without it. And thanks to it, a mind blowing diversity of creatures thrive beneath the surface. Born to enjoy the riches underwater they must master their body's adaptations, learn to find food, to escape danger and above all become expert swimmers! Whether it's in rivers, on beaches or in the deep ocean, all must overcome the obstacles and fulfil their destiny, all are born to swim! Of all the habitats on earth, the ocean is perhaps the most challenging. Here salt, temperatures, currents and predators can make life difficult; it's not an obvious place to want to bring up your babies… But even in these waters generation after generation beats the odds. They have overcome the challenges and become some of the most beautiful and graceful of creatures. But any baby born to swim has a lot to learn… All over the world there are creatures born to swim, and though humans are not, we seem determined to join them. Perhaps we are envious of their grace. Maybe we are all water babies at heart

programme.title: New Life
programme.desc: Baby animals beguile with their innocent charm. But while some remain harmless, others have a deep-rooted instinct. It may take weeks or months of training to hone their skills. Their landscapes and lifestyles maybe very different, but these babies are born to hunt.

programme.title: The U.S. East Coast
programme.sub-title: The U.S. East Coast, Florida - The Sunshine State
programme.desc: Nowhere is the American way of life as relaxed as on Florida's beaches. The Keys, a chain of islands at the far southern end of the state, reveal a more laid-back side of the USA. But all of Florida amazes -- with secluded beaches, unspoiled nature, and the legacy of the early European settlers.

programme.title: The U.S. East Coast
programme.sub-title: The U.S. East Coast, The Heart of New England
programme.desc: From New York, the journey up the coast leads into the heart of New England. We visit Yale; spend some time in the 19th century mansions at Newport, RI; tour the coastal fishing and vacation towns of Massachusetts, and then meet a modern entrepreneur in Boston and see her rooftop gardens.

programme.title: The U.S. East Coast
programme.sub-title: The U.S. East Coast, Surf And Turf
programme.desc: New Hampshire has only 19 kilometers of coastline and short summers, all the more reason Shauna Fraser takes every opportunity to be by the sea. In the sheltered harbor of Rye, Shauna also targets inner attitude with yoga on a paddleboard.

programme.title: Wildlife Nannies
programme.desc: Dolphin Joan: Dolphin Joan is the darling of visitors to the “Loro Parque” and will face the challenge of his first live show in front of an audience. His trainer Miguel Luis Sosa is going through the final training lessons with him. Will this quick learner be able to perform with the help of his mother and grandmother? Vervet Monkey Alfie: Silke von Eynern is runs the “Bambelela Farm” in South Africa, where she has taken care of vervet monkeys for many years. Her newest arrival is Alfie, a vervet monkey that has been kept as a pet and been treated all wrong. Silke is on a mission to teach 10 weeks old Alfie how to be a proper monkey. Wildlife Foundlings: It’s high season at the wild animal station Sachsenhagen. Mareen Möller takes care of all sorts of foundlings and injured orphans -- she has her hands full all day. Especially a baby fox named Didi and a wild boar baby named Armin require all her attention as they are growing tall and bold.

programme.title: Wildlife Nannies
programme.desc: Tree-Kangaroo Dorothy: Margit Cianelli is a trained animal carer who lives at the Lumholtz Lodge in the Australian tablelands and is currently taking care of small tree kangaroo named Dorothy. Dorothy is old and strong enough to go into the forest by herself now. Margit gets nervous, when Dorothy does not return before dusk. Luckily she is wearing a tracking device… Warthog Baby: Nelly is a 5 week old Warthog that lives in the Kragga Kamma Game Park in South Africa. Her nanny Heike Zitzer is trying to wean Nelly from her care, but the little warthog just loves being by her side. When Heike leaves her alone, Nelly meets grumpy fellows as she searches for Heike throughout the park. Will Nelly make new friends in the end?

programme.title: Wildlife Nannies
programme.desc: Arctic Wolves: A pack of 5 week old wolf cubs get ready to go to school with famous film animal trainer Joe Bodemann. But before that, the little cubs have to be weaned off their bottles and get used to their new outdoor enclosure. Wildlife Warriors: These special nannies are always ready to jump into action to save animals of every kind. Over 100 patients are being treated by this Australian wildlife hospital every day: tortoises, kookaburras, wild boars, possums, hedgehogs - and even poisonous snakes! Lion Amir: In South Africa, 11 month old lion Amir was brought to his nanny Katja Peters half starved. Now he’s maturing into a beautiful lion and is looking for company. Will he find true love with a very special lioness?

programme.title: Wildlife Nannies
programme.desc: Raccoon Zorro: 8 week old racoon Zorro is a real handful for Nanny Julia Wiertz. No stone is left unturned on his mischievous adventures. Will Zorro be able to learn how to climb and scavage for food with Julia as a teacher? Storks: Dr. Michael Kaatz has two motherless baby storks at his stork sanctuary. In order to find a new home for Max and Moritz, he is trying to trick a stork family into adopting the orphaned storks. But will the recovered foundlings ever be able to fly in the wild? Zebra Baby et al.: Wildlife keeper Jaco Nell teaches his new assistant Anna de la Roca how to take care of cheetahs, rhinos and zebras on his South African gaming reserve “Kapama”.

programme.title: Wildlife Nannies
programme.desc: Emus: The two emu babies Candy and Henry have accidentally imprinted on their care taker Matthias Reinschmidt at the “Loro Parque” in Tenerife. Now the two young birds follow Matthias everywhere he goes. But they are now big enough to live in an outdoor enclosure of the park – alongside giant turtles! Will they accept their new home and the fact that Matthias will only come to visit sporadically from now on? Baboon Babies: In Namibia, Corinna Auffing and her husband John Ross Stewart are teaching the three little baboon babies Drekki, Stocki and Beuki everything they need to know in order to become proper baboons. Elephant Limpopo: Elephant Baby Limpopo joins the herd on a South African elephant safari under the guidance of Nanny Paul Coetzee for the first time.

programme.title: Wildlife Nannies
programme.desc: Pilot Nanny: At the South African “Sondéla Wildlife Center” nanny Petronel Nieuwoudt takes care of animals that have been injured or orphaned because of poaching. Her newest “problem child” is a gnu named Cabrona – will she find a new family for the shy animal? Penguins: Nanny Wolfgang Magnus is responsible for the penguin offspring in Germany’s famous bird park in Walsrode. He spends all the time he can with them, makes sure they gain enough weight and even builds them little caves made out of paper-mâché – several times daily!

programme.title: Dreams of the Settlers - An American Journey
programme.sub-title: Through Utah's Canyons to Salt Lake City
programme.desc: It’s a dream route leading from the casino metropolis of Las Vegas, to Salt Lake City, the Mormon capital. It is a journey that follows the footsteps of the first settlers: the Mormons. They came to this desert belt of the United States during the second half of the 19th century to escape persecution and in search of the promised land. Some of them founded Las Vegas. Others moved through the canyons and over the mountains of the colossal Colorado Plateau, where they found water and pasture, to establish many small settlements. The landscapes through which they trekked, had and still have, a breathtaking and bizarre majestic beauty. Bryce Canyon, the hogbacks and the Escalante Staircase plateau are dramatic scenes of wonder formed from rock. The Pajute Indians once described Bryce Canyon as: “red rocks standing like men in a basin”.

programme.title: Dreams of the Settlers - An American Journey
programme.desc: The vast Delta of the Mississippi River is the gateway to the South of the United States. It always has been. This is where our journey begins: through Louisiana along the banks of the mighty river, to the big inland city of New Orleans, founded long ago by the French. Further inland, sugarcane plantations and the villas of their former owners stretch as far as the eye can see. To the north, we leave the river and, taking spectacular roads, we pass through the swamplands of the Mississippi, which were once impenetrable, serving as the ideal hideout for many a runaway. Bordering Texas, where the swamplands end, is the beginning of the vast cattle country. This is home to the last remaining cowboys in the United States who, still today, maintain their French cultural heritage.

programme.title: Dreams of the Settlers - An American Journey
programme.sub-title: Along the Shores of the Great Lakes
programme.desc: The Great Lakes are among the most mythical landscapes of North America: endless water, endless forest. Many Native American peoples lived there; later European settlers followed on the heels of the white hunters and trappers. They hunted in the forests and transported their furs and other goods across the water to the south where a small river situated at the southern tip of Lake Michigan flows onward towards the southern states. The settlement that sprung up there was called Checagou by the Indians. Much later, the settlement was to be the first skyscraper city in the world: Chicago, the megacity on the Great Lake.

programme.title: mareTV: The Eckernförde Bay - Beach shooters, mine clearance divers, squirrels
programme.desc: Fjord, ford, bay, gulf: there are many names for the beautiful extensions of the Baltic Sea into the country. Eckernförde Bay is shaped by currents, surf and people. It stretches 17 kilometers to the town that gives it its name. Stephan Möller is the acting shooting master of the Eckernförder Bürgerschützengilde. He spent a whole year preparing the beach shooting festival and then this: fog, rain and a stiff breeze. Traditionally, the Eckernförde marksmen always shoot at a wooden bird in the direction of the sea at Whitsun. Under strict conditions! And these stipulate a clear view of at least one kilometer out into the bay. The event literally threatens to fall through, for the first time in 449 years. Around 3,000 soldiers are stationed at the Eckernförde naval base, but only a small group is allowed to call itself an elite unit: the mine divers. They are considered the toughest soldiers in the Navy. Anyone who wants to be one of them has to undergo extreme training. This year, just two candidates made it to the final round. Now they still have to pass the dreaded final exam: running 20 kilometers with marching gear and then swimming ten kilometers through Eckernförde Bay.

programme.title: mareTV: Wismar Bay - World Heritage and Water Paradise
programme.desc: The Wismar Bay is unique in its diversity on the Mecklenburg Baltic Sea coast: cliffs, sandy beaches, salt marshes, enchanted islands and, last but not least, the proud Hanseatic city of Wismar with its brick Gothic buildings, which are a UNESCO World Heritage Site. When the mighty logs get wedged together in the timber mill in the port of Wismar, Steven Buchholz has to get to work with his chainsaw. It's a dangerous job, because the logs weigh tons. As a trained carpenter, Steven is a virtuoso with the chain saw. Even after his shift is over, he is looking for the thrill: the wind off his home island of Poel is favorable that day and he wants to reclaim the German record in the kite surfing high jump. For a long time, Ramona Stelzer only knew fish as a tasty ingredient between two halves of a bun. But when the goldsmith got her hands on fish leather, her business idea was born: she processes the unique natural product fish skin into fine jewelry. The problem is that hardly anyone in Germany tans fish skin. In order to reliably obtain supplies, Ramona now wants to learn this craft herself.

programme.title: Force of Nature Rewind
programme.desc: Wild and viral weather footage from across Canada and around the world

programme.title: Force of Nature Rewind
programme.desc: Wild and viral weather footage from across Canada and around the world

programme.title: Survivorman
programme.desc: The key to daily survival here is finding food in a jungle renowned for its poisonous insects and reptiles.

programme.title: Survivorman
programme.desc: Leopards, snakes, territorial honey bees and the infamous Royal Bengal tiger all pose threats to Les survival.

programme.title: What If
programme.desc: Take an epic journey through hypothetical worlds and possibilities.

programme.title: How To Survive
programme.desc: Endure life-threatening moments by employing statistical facts and survivor stories, all while entertaining.

programme.title: Survivorman: Bigfoot
programme.desc: Les tracks Sasquatch from East Texas to rural Utah in an effort to elicit an encounter.

programme.title: Dream In Colour
programme.desc: Get lost in the breathtaking beauty of Canadian and international landscapes

programme.title: Bucketlist South America
programme.desc: One of the biggest continents crammed with adventures and possibilities, South America should be up there on any wildlife watcher's bucket list. From record breaking rivers and longest mountain ranges to the steepest waterfalls and most massive rainforests, the variety of landscapes supports a seemingly endless diversity of wildlife. Here you can find the biggest rodents, otters and parrots, with unique mammals and marine life and spectacular predators. Make sure you don't miss South America!

programme.title: Fight for Life
programme.desc: Nature is red in tooth and claw – animals face daily battles to protect themselves and secure the resources they need to survive. For predators to find food, lower links in the food chain have a fight on their hands escaping the jaws of death. Their only options are to die, run or turn and fight. Winning these battles will make the difference between life and death, and with the stakes so high don't be surprised if the biggest animals don't have everything their own way. Welcome to nature's fight club.

programme.title: Fighting for a Mate
programme.desc: Animals face daily battles to protect themselves and secure the resources they need to survive. Every battle is a trade-off between the risk to health and the prizes awarded to the victors. Each animal fights with a unique set of tools, from display structures and elaborate body language to claws, jaws and antlers. And few fights are as fuelled as those during breeding season the ultimate trophy for the brave fighter is, of course, a chance to pass on the fighting genes to the next generation.

programme.title: Destination Wild: Wild Chile
programme.desc: This is the story of an awe-inspiring land of extremes. From the cold and fearsome waters of Tierra Del Fuego and the Antarctic Peninsula to the frozen Patagonian steppe, we follow the animals living on the very edge of existence, having to survive is some of the most extreme environments on earth. King Penguins court on the windswept coast leopard seals hunt amongst the ice floes and young pumas take their first steps toward independence is this South American final Frontier.

programme.title: Destination Wild: Wild Chile
programme.desc: Chile is at the mercy of weather systems that have shaped the nation. In the South, strong westerlies bring rains that create rich green Valdivian forests, carpeting the neotropic ecozone. The Valdivian forests of Chile are a unique ecosystem harbouring species of a ‘land before time' – The forests of Valdivia represent the unique assemblage of ancient species, both floral and faunal, which today persist as relicts of Gondwanaland.

programme.title: Destination Wild: Wild Chile
programme.desc: Chile is at the mercy of weather systems that have shaped the nation. In the South, strong westerlies bring rains that create rich green Valdivian forests, carpeting the neotropic ecozone. The Valdivian forests of Chile are a unique ecosystem harbouring species of a ‘land before time' – The forests of Valdivia represent the unique assemblage of ancient species, both floral and faunal, which today persist as relicts of Gondwanaland.

programme.title: Destination Wild: Wild Chile
programme.desc: Chile is a ribbon of land trapped by natural borders. At its Northern edge, the driest desert in the world cuts off the country from the rest of South America. Here, in the Atacama desert, wildlife has spent millions of years adapting to a life without water. Those that survive have learnt that no opportunity comes without its fair share of risk, but for the animals of the Atacama, only those that dare, will make it in the most unforgiving desert on Earth.

programme.title: Secret Life Of The Circler
programme.desc: This film reveals the journey of the rare and beautiful raptor, the black harrier, from egg to fledging and the dramatic and fascinating role of the female while nesting, a time when the mother and her chicks are at their most vulnerable to predators

programme.title: Modern Migrations
programme.desc: The capture team is busy in Ithala Game Reserve where several warthogs need new pastures. Meanwhile an interesting small animal, called a 'dassie' or rock hyrax is lured into traps as they are becoming a menace in the Ntshondwe camp area.

programme.title: Modern Migrations
programme.desc: Being one of Africa's Big 5, the Buffalo is an animal that requires a well-experienced team to capture and load. Bushbuck on the other hand proves to be much easier to capture...

programme.title: All The Presidents Elephants
programme.desc: Sharon Pincott, from Queensland, Australia, ditches her friends and material possessions to pursue a dangerous life in Robert Mugabe's Zimbabwe - as caretaker of the 'presidential herd' of elephants.

programme.title: Race of Life
programme.desc: Deserts, harsh and arid, challenge life with scant rainfall. Desert animals adapt to conserve water, relying on food for hydration. They seek shade, burrow, or became nocturnal to survive extreme temperatures.

programme.title: Race of Life
programme.desc: Elephants, thrive on vegetation, though they're not averse to confrontation. Rhinos, resilient yet endangered, face threats from predators and humanity. Hippos, formidable and protective, safeguard their young from crocodiles and other predators.

programme.title: Shark Dive TV
programme.sub-title: Zebra Sharks in the Indian Ocean
programme.desc: Shark Dive TV searches for Zebra Sharks in the Indian Ocean, features amazing shark performances in the Philippines and does a Deeper Dive by examining shark finning and Thresher Sharks.

programme.title: Super Senses
programme.desc: In this episode, we examine how every animal has what could be called a sense of rhythm - the human animal included. To give an obvious example, there's our internal body clock which responds to the so-called Circadian rhythm.

programme.title: Super Senses
programme.desc: While all life began in the oceans, human beings now rely on technology to navigate the seven seas - left to our own devices we're like the proverbial fish out of water. Only the other way around.

programme.title: City Zoo
programme.desc: In this episode we explore the zoo's satellite breeding facility, the Mokopane Biodiversity and Conservation Centre. We meet Johannes, part of the zoo crew and see why he has such a special relationship with the animals he cares for.

programme.title: Lucky Dog Reunions
programme.desc: Before being placed with an active and social couple, a rescued poodle mix must demonstrate an interest in the great outdoors and a knack for party etiquette.

programme.title: Wild Child
programme.desc: Sheinelle Jones takes us to Africa to explore the family bonds of the animals who live in the Savannah.

programme.title: Wild Child
programme.desc: Sheinelle Jones takes us on a journey around North America to find the most fascinating families in the wild.

programme.title: Wild Child
programme.desc: Sheinelle Jones takes us on a journey around the globe and to explore some of our favorite beach babies on the planet.

programme.title: Wild Child
programme.desc: Sheinelle Jones takes us on a journey around the world to explore the coldest places with the cutest families.

programme.title: Wild Child
programme.desc: Sheinelle Jones takes us on a journey around the planet to explore some of the hottest places that some of the cutest animal families call home.

programme.title: Wild Child
programme.desc: Sheinelle Jones takes us on an exploration around the globe to find our favorite animal families of summer.

programme.title: Wild Child
programme.desc: Sheinelle Jones takes us on a journey around the world to see how these animal families survive and thrive in one of the coolest seasons

programme.title: Wild Child
programme.desc: Sheinelle Jones leads us on an adventure to the African Plains to discover some of our favorite animal kids and their safari siblings.

programme.title: KITTY 911
programme.desc: Meet the VOKRA volunteers who work tirelessly to rescue Vancouver�s kitties. Karen, assisted by student vet Clare, vaccinates a litter of kittens, Maria is called out to a rescue and an angry Agatha is dropped off at the Operations Centre after being found in her carrier in an alley.

programme.title: KITTY 911
programme.desc: Maria continues her efforts to trap the "Mattress Kittens", Karen is forced to face her worst fears about Yvette, we visit former feral Shandy, now in long-term foster-care and Milo finds his forever home.

programme.title: Lucky Dog Reunions
programme.desc: Two previous adopters share success stories that detail what life has been like for former ranch residents Lady and Winnie.

programme.title: Lucky Dog Reunions
programme.desc: A recently rescued spaniel mix could go home with an active couple, but before the match can be made, Finn needs to work on becoming the ultimate adventure buddy.

programme.title: Lucky Dog Reunions
programme.desc: Eric and Rashi receive positive progress reports from the adopters of former ranch residents Leo and Reggie.
